title: When AI Meets Government Power, the "Thin Line" Gets Thinner
excerpt: The Anthropic-U.S. clash shows how fast AI is becoming a strategic government-controlled domain.

title: The Real Cost of AI. The Cost of AI Is Not Money; It Is Control.
excerpt: The true cost of AI adoption is not subscriptions or tokens; it is long-term control over your institutional reasoning.

title: AI Is Not Replacing Jobs; It Is Redefining What Work Actually Looks Like.
excerpt: AI is not removing work. It is shifting professional value from manual execution to judgment, direction, and leverage.

title: My Thoughts on the Near Future of AI
excerpt: The near future of AI is not full human replacement. It is practical human-AI partnership with stronger output and oversight.

title: How Have We Quietly Given Up on Privacy Because It Is Convenient
excerpt: Convenience-first AI habits are quietly normalizing oversharing and blurring the line between fast workflows and safe data handling.

title: Does AI Still Belong to Silicon Valley?
excerpt: AI is reducing the startup advantage of physical location by amplifying small teams and enabling faster product execution from anywhere.
